title: Suspected infringement on Disney property rights [TVB show in Hong Kong]

Dear Sir/Madam,

I am writing to report a suspected infringement on your company's property rights as appeared in a TV show produced by a local broadcasting company "Television Broadcasts Limited" ("TVB") in the Hong Kong region.

The suspected infringement appeared in the 611st episode named "The Spoilers" ("劇透者聯盟") of show "Come Home Love: Lo and Behold" (愛回家之開心速遞). It was aired on July 1st, 2019 on the channel named "TVB Jade" in the Hong Kong Television network. More, It was also broadcasted on its online platform. The related trailer for that particular show is still available on Youtube. (https://youtu.be/z6rTvAwW9Rw)

The show itself showed an unacceptable resemblance to the Avengers 4 series with reference to the figures, images, graphic designs and digital effects. The use of Disney characters was excessive and It is hardly a creation out of the Disney framework and storyline. Therefore, It aroused my concern on whether TVB have obtained prior permission before portraying the characters from the Avengers, or is it merely a steal from your company.

TVB is the largest publicly-traded broadcasting company in Hong Kong with 86% to the audience share and they have every responsibility to ensure zero piracies in their works. I feel deeply disturbed and worried on seeing such obvious and overwhelming exploitation to the Disney's intellectual rights.

I am a huge supporter of Disney works. It is a shame for TVB to be ignoring the basis of creational rights and having no respect to ownership of copyrights and trademarks. As such, I am looking forward to hearing from you for an investigation on the above issue.

Thank you for your attention.

Yours faithfully

香港人

infringements@marvel.com
tips@disneyantipiracy.com

Suspected infringement on Disney property rights

Dear Sir/Madam,

I am writing to report a suspected infringement on your company's property rights as appeared in a TV show produced by a local broadcasting company "Television Broadcasts Limited" ("TVB") in Hong Kong.

The suspected infringement appeared in the 611th episode named "The Spoilers Alliance" (translated from"劇透者聯盟", a play on words on "The Avengers", "復仇者聯盟" in Chinese) of show "Come Home Love: Lo and Behold" (愛回家之開心速遞). It was aired on July 1st, 2019 on the channel named "TVB Jade" in the Hong Kong Television network. It was also broadcasted on its online platform.

The related trailer for that particular show is still available on TVB's official YouTube channel "TVB (official)".

https://youtu.be/z6rTvAwW9Rw

A clip of the actual TV show is also available on the TVB's USA YouTube channel "TVB USA Official".

https://youtu.be/VB3YvQ35Qbg

From 0:49 of the clip, one can see characters that bear striking resemblance to Marvel's Thor, Scarlet Witch, Gamora, Hulk, Captain Marvel, Hawkeye, Captain American, and Iron Man. At 1:15, the character resembling Iron Man said in Cantonese: "報仇者，集結！", which translates to "Revengers, Assemble!".

The show itself showed an unacceptable resemblance to the Avengers 4 series with reference to the figures, images, dialogue, graphic designs and digital effects. The use of Disney characters was excessive and It is hardly a creation out of the Disney framework and storyline. Therefore, It aroused my concern on whether TVB have obtained prior permission before portraying the characters from the Avengers, or is it merely a steal from your company.

TVB is the largest publicly-traded broadcasting company in Hong Kong with 86% to the audience share and they have every responsibility to ensure zero piracies in their works. I feel deeply disturbed and worried on seeing such obvious and overwhelming exploitation to the Disney's intellectual rights.

I am a huge supporter of Disney works and a big Marvel fan. It is a shame for TVB to be ignoring the basis of creational rights and having no respect to ownership of copyrights and trademarks. As such, I am looking forward to hearing from you for an investigation on the above issue.

Thank you for your attention.

Yours faithfully,

It is arguable that what TVB did exceeded the scope of fair use as substantial commercial interest is involved with no prima facie indication of transformative use.
